hawkish_phrases: President George dissented because she believed that an unchanged setting of policy was appropriate
President Rosengren dissented because he judged that monetary policy was already accommodative
additional accommodation was not needed for an economy in which labor markets are already tight
could pose risks of further inflating the prices of risky assets and encouraging households and firms to take on too much leverage

Several participants suggested that the expectations regarding the path of the federal funds rate implied by prices in financial markets were currently suggesting greater provision of accommodation at coming meetings than they saw as appropriate
dovish_phrases: President Bullard dissented because he believed that lowering the target range for the federal funds rate by 50 basis points at this time would provide insurance against further declines in expected inflation
business fixed investment and exports have weakened
downside risks to the outlook for economic activity had increased somewhat since their July meeting
market-based measures of inflation compensation remained low; survey-based measures of longer-term inflation expectations were near historical lows

This action supports the Committee's view that sustained expansion of economic activity, strong labor market conditions, and inflation near the Committee's symmetric 2 percent objective are the most likely outcomes, but uncertainties about this outlook remain
summary: The Committee voted 7-3 to cut the federal funds rate by 25 basis points to 1-3/4 to 2 percent, with Bullard dissenting for a larger 50bp cut and George and Rosengren dissenting against any cut, reflecting a mildly dovish action motivated by trade uncertainty, weak investment, and below-target inflation despite a strong labor market.
